goal: Fix: gaps reports false-uncovered when git fetch is fresher than the materialized view

summary.title: Fix: gaps auto-syncs so a fresh fetch is reflected against the view
summary.what: Add `gaps` to autoSyncCommands so `mainline gaps` runs a sync (gated by Sync.FreshnessSeconds, default 300s) before reading coverage. Previously gaps read the last 30 commits on the synced main ref against the local view's intent list — but when `git fetch` had just brought in a new merge commit + its mainline note, the corresponding intent was not yet in view.Intents, so liveIntents did not include it, and the note's reference did not match. The commit reported as uncovered even though the team-truth said it was covered. After the fix, gaps prints `Syncing with team...` first when the view is older than the freshness window, rebuilds, and reports correctly.
summary.why: User reported running `./mainline gaps` and seeing a commit by another actor (`f42ab6e8 — fix: improve hub local reader navigation`) flagged as uncovered. Investigation: the commit + its mainline note were on the local refs (note added_by another actor, fetched via the configured refspec), but the view rebuild had not run since the fetch, so the intent was not in liveIntents. This is the same false-uncovered staleness pattern that already pushed `status` and `check` into autoSyncCommands; gaps was the third member of the same family that had been missed.
summary.user_goal: Make `mainline gaps` give an answer that matches what `mainline sync && mainline gaps` would give, without forcing the user to remember the two-step.

decisions.rationale: Same fix as `status` and `check`. Auto-sync is gated by the 300s freshness window so repeated calls stay cheap, and `--no-sync` remains for the script-friendly opt-out path. One-line change at the integration boundary, no behavioural change to coverage logic.

decisions.chose: Keep the strict 'note ⇒ intent in liveIntents AND non-abandoned' check in CoverageWindow.
decisions.rationale: The abandoned filter is the real safety net: a sealed-then-abandoned intent should not silently keep its commit marked covered. Relaxing the check would trade one rare bug (post-seal abandonment) for fixing a bug auto-sync already addresses.

decisions.chose: Only add `gaps`. Leave `context`, `list-proposals`, `log` alone for now.
decisions.rationale: Each command has its own staleness vs. cost trade-off (`log` has --sync, `context` is hot-path for agents). Bundling them into one PR mixes user-driven evidence (gaps was reported wrong today) with speculation (the others might be wrong tomorrow).

rejected.alternative: Detect view-vs-fetched-ref staleness and force a sync regardless of freshness window.
rejected.reason: Bigger change, would surprise users who just synced and ran gaps repeatedly. Auto-sync gated by freshness keeps the existing model intact.

rejected.alternative: Drop the abandoned-intent filter from coverage, treating any valid note as covered.
rejected.reason: Removes a real safety net for sealed-then-abandoned intents. Auto-sync solves the same user-reported case without weakening coverage semantics.

rejected.alternative: Print a `view is stale` warning instead of forcing a sync.
rejected.reason: Warning + wrong answer is worse than slightly slower correct answer. The 300s budget already caps the cost.

risks: Within the freshness window (last sync < 300s ago), gaps still skips the sync — so a manual `git fetch` between two gaps calls could still produce a stale answer. Acceptable: --no-sync remains the opt-out, and the typical agent loop syncs much more often than every 5 minutes.
followups: Audit `context`, `list-proposals` for the same staleness pattern as the user dogfoods Hub v1; add to autoSyncCommands if they exhibit it.
Consider a `view-vs-ref staleness` signal in `mainline status` (warn when the view rebuilt before the most recent fetch on origin/main).

architectural_claims: Read commands whose answer depends on the materialized view's freshness must auto-sync by default; --no-sync remains the per-call opt-out.
Auto-sync gating belongs at the CLI boundary (autoSyncCommands map), not inside engine read paths.
behavioral_changes: `mainline gaps` now triggers an auto-sync (subject to Sync.FreshnessSeconds, default 300s) before reading coverage. Output unchanged when the view is fresh.

api_changes.signature: mainline gaps now auto-syncs (gated by freshness window)
